div1.entryFree: Popular, vulgar, plebeian: that which, but by being so retired, o'erprized all p. rate, Tp. I, 2, 92. art thou officer? or art thou base, common and p.? H5 IV, 1, 38. seld-shown flamens do press among the p. throngs and puff to win a vulgar station, Cor. II, 1, 230. I will counterfeit the bewitchment of some p. man and give it bountiful to the desirers, II, 3, 109. who puts his Shall, his p. Shall, against a graver bench than ever frowned in Greece, III, 1, 106. and in a violent p. ignorance, given your enemy your shield, V, 2, 43.
